pi_items.source: Verified in Bridge Command source code (main.cpp): view_angle is parsed from bc5.ini as Horizontal Field of View parameter. Confirmed in bc5.ini config file in [Graphics] section.
Verified in bc5.ini line 13: view_angle=90. Also verified in setup_task.sh line 24 which resets view_angle to 90 before task execution using sed command.

Verified by source code analysis (main.cpp and Camera.cpp). Any value > 0 is valid. 60 degrees is a valid field of view angle. Source code confirms: higher values = wider FOV, lower values = narrower FOV. 60 is narrower than 90. Formula in Camera.cpp: vFOV = 2*atan(tan(hFOV/2)/aspect)

Logical state based on task description: changing view_angle from 90 to 60 and saving results in modified settings. Verifier is a stub (verifier.py returns always True) with external VLM evaluation, so this state name is reasonable.

privileged_info_summary: The task configure_simulation_settings@1 uses Bridge Command, an open-source ship bridge simulator. The task requires changing the graphics parameter 'view_angle' from 90 degrees (default, wider field of view) to 60 degrees (narrower field of view) via the settings editor. The view_angle parameter is the Horizontal Field of View setting read from bc5.ini in the [Graphics] section. All metadata values are correct and consistent with Bridge Command source code and configuration files.
